title: Dactylicapnos paucinervia
taxonomy: Dactylicapnos paucinervia is classified in the family Papaveraceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as poppies. It is further classified within the genus Dactylicapnos, which is a small genus of flowering plants native to the Himalayas. The plant is also known as Himalayan capnoides or snow poppy, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations. As a member of the Papaveraceae family, Dactylicapnos paucinervia shares similarities with other poppy species in terms of its flower structure and growth habits. The classification of Dactylicapnos paucinervia provides valuable insights into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.

intro: Dactylicapnos paucinervia, also known as Himalayan bleeding heart or Dactylicapnos, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its unique, heart-shaped flowers that secrete a red, sap-like substance. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dactylicapnos paucinervia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dactylicapnos is often prepared as a decoction or powder for use in treating respiratory disorders and is also used in some traditional formulas to promote digestion and alleviate symptoms of cold and cough.
